title: Deterioration of stone materials in the Angkor monuments, Cambodia
highlights: famous examples of Cultural Heritage constructed between the 9th and 13th centuries and were registered on the World Heritage List in 1992. The monuments have suffered various forms of deterioration and the need for restoration is urgent. The restoration of the Angkor monuments was initiated by the EFEO (l'École Française d'Extrême Orient) in 1907 and was followed by organization from other countries including India, Indonesia, Italy, Canada, Hungary and the USA. The Japanese Government organized the Japanese Government Team for  ...    investigations. This study was financially supported in part by the UNESCO/Japanese Trust Fund for the Preservation of the World Cultural Heritage.  ...    Show abstract  The Angkor monuments have been registered on the World Cultural Heritage List of UNESCO, while the buildings built mostly of sandstone are suffering from serious deterioration and damage.
